response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise and demonstrated systems-level thinking through low-latency microservices and data pipeline work, directly relevant to the routing domain. However, the candidate's C++ experience is stale (last professional work 2014-2015) with a decade-long trajectory toward full-stack TypeScript development. While their D language and embedded systems background shows systems programming capability, assessing ramp-up speed on modern C++ and routing-specific optimization would be essential before advancing.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective platform and Green Map work, directly applicable to routing tile systems and navigation data
Demonstrated low-latency systems thinking from Dunnhumby role handling millions of requests/sec using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Systems programming background with C++, D, embedded work, and strong emphasis on testing, TDD, and data pipeline design
concerns: C++ expertise is outdated; last professional C++ work was 2014-2015 at National Instruments; no evidence of modern C++11/14/17 or recent systems programming in production
No demonstrated routing or navigation systems experience; geospatial background is data collection and mapping rather than routing optimization and guidance
Career trajectory for past decade diverged toward full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React), away from systems-level programming required for critical infrastructure
